title: Golden Globes: Paul Thomas Anderson Honors Warner Bros.' Michael De Luca After Winning Best Director
text: Director Paul Thomas Anderson won the Golden Globe for Best Director at the 2026 ceremony for his film 'One Battle After Another.' He also took home the award for Best Screenplay for a Motion Picture that same evening. During his acceptance speech, Anderson made a point of crediting Michael De Luca, co-chair and CEO of Warner Bros. Motion Picture Group, saying 'he single-handedly has supported me and the movies that I wanted to make.' Anderson described De Luca's decades-long support, explaining that approximately 25-30 years ago, De Luca became his champion with aspirations to run a studio while allowing directors creative freedom. The director also honored his late assistant director Adam Somner, a frequent collaborator who passed away in 2024, noting that Somner's final project was 'One Battle After Another' and includes a dedication to him. Supporting actress Teyana Taylor also won that evening for her role in the picture.
